title: The Call
summary: The crew of the Leviathan — Draven Frost, D-Volt, and Echo — assembled at Station Nowhere and took a job from Syndicate broker Mira Vex. Target: the MSV Carrion Star, a freighter that went dark 72 hours ago in the Margins. After hard negotiation and a passive scan of Mira's comm channel that revealed a Church relay node in the signal chain, the crew secured 100,000 credits and a full defensive resupply. They folded out and are now on approach to the Carrion Star's last known position.
location: Station Nowhere → Transit → Approach to MSV Carrion Star

objectives.primary: Retrieve cargo from MSV Carrion Star — sealed lead-lined container, nav core, personal effects crate
new: Recover personal effects crate — tagged RENNER, T.

key_events: Crew assembled at the Fore observation blister aboard the Leviathan at Station Nowhere
Mira Vex opened comms — offered 50k for a 'clean' salvage job on the Carrion Star
Draven pushed back — crew noted no mention of the Carrion Star's nine crew
D-Volt ran a passive Warfare Suite scan on Mira's comm channel (roll: 11, success with complication)
Scan revealed: signal bounced through Church of the Architects relay node; stress micropattern on the word 'cargo'; the scan itself was passively logged by something on the relay chain
Cargo manifest reviewed — priority item is a 480kg lead-lined sealed container marked DO NOT SCAN
Draven negotiated from 50k → 80k → walked → Mira called back in 40 seconds → 100k + full resupply
Resupply at Station Nowhere: medical kit restock, breach kits, signal jammer, PDW for Echo, survey drone, thermal breaching tape
Leviathan folded out — 9-hour transit to Carrion Star position
Now on approach — Carrion Star visible on sensors, running lights off, drifting
npcs_introduced: Mira Vex — Syndicate broker, job source. Controlled, professional, knew the job wasn't clean. Client unknown — referred to as 'my client', not 'the Syndicate'.
loot_acquired: Resupply package (medical, breach kits, signal jammer, PDW, survey drone, thermal tape) — covered by Mira's provisioner credit

player_choices.choice: Draven walked away from 90k and forced Mira to call back
player_choices.outcome: Secured 100k — Mira moved in under 40 seconds, confirming she expected to be pushed
player_choices.future_implications: Mira knows this crew negotiates hard. Respect or annoyance — TBD.

player_choices.choice: D-Volt ran a passive scan on Mira's comm channel
player_choices.outcome: Discovered Church relay node in signal chain; confirmed Mira knows what's in the cargo; the scan was logged by something on the relay
player_choices.future_implications: Something in the Church infrastructure now knows the Leviathan was curious. This will surface.

player_choices.choice: Crew accepted the job despite red flags
player_choices.outcome: 100k on delivery, en route to a dark ship with unknown cargo and a client Mira won't name
player_choices.future_implications: The Carrion Star is waiting. Whatever happened to nine crew is still in there.

cliffhanger: Leviathan on approach to MSV Carrion Star — running lights off, drifting, silent. Survey drone ready. Crew assembled at the Fore. Whatever is on that ship is about to become their problem.
gm_notes: D-Volt's scan was logged — seed this later, Church agent Brother Cassius may already know the Leviathan is coming. Mira's 'my client' distinction is intentional — client is Church-adjacent, not Syndicate. Echo-VEGA divergence established as canon (Option B). D-Volt claimed manual gunnery station. The sealed container is the Resonance Key. DO NOT reveal until dramatically appropriate.

quotes.speaker: VEGA
quotes.quote: She said 'clean.' I'm logging that for later.

quotes.speaker: Draven Frost
quotes.quote: Ace in the hole Mira, you know damn well that data core is blackbox class. 100k and some guns.

quotes.speaker: D-Volt
quotes.quote: Well first I gotta go drain some oil.

quotes.speaker: Echo
quotes.quote: I don't know which one that means.
